browse_category.description: Flat human browse bucket (e.g., knowledge, engineering, quality). Renamed from v2 `family`. For hierarchical taxonomy use `category` instead.

category.description: Hierarchical browse path using slash-delimited segments (e.g., `ecommerce/integrations/shopify`). Complements `browse_category`; flat vs tree answer different questions.

last_verified.description: ISO date of the last verification against truth sources. Authored claim.

truth_source_hashes.description: Map of truth source file path → SHA-256 hex digest at the time of last verification. Evidence that complements the `last_verified` claim. Computed by `skill-graph drift-check --record`.

superseded_by.description: Name of the skill that replaces this one. Required when `stability: deprecated` — enforced by the allOf rule so every deprecated skill names its successor and consumers can follow the chain automatically. Omit on non-deprecated skills.

runtimes.description: Target agent runtimes with optional version constraints (e.g., `claude-code>=2.0`, `cursor>=0.40`).

notes.description: Free-text additional compatibility notes. Use sparingly; prefer structured fields.

triggers.description: Exact phrase or label triggers that activate this skill. For semantic phrases use `keywords`; for example user prompts use `examples`.

keywords.description: Semantic keywords for discovery and fuzzy matching. Complements `triggers` (exact) and `examples` (full prompts).

examples.description: Positive-class activation examples — realistic user prompts the skill SHOULD activate for. 2–5 entries recommended. Improves recall for embedding-based routers (SkillRouter-style few-shot retrieval) that find `keywords` alone insufficient at library scale. Groups under `activation.examples` in the manifest.

anti_examples.description: Negative-class activation examples — realistic user prompts that look topically related but a DIFFERENT skill should handle. Used as hard-negative training signal. Pair with `relations.boundary` to name the skill that should activate instead. Groups under `activation.anti_examples` in the manifest.

paths.description: Glob patterns that identify code surfaces this skill governs. Patterns prefixed with `!` are negations (gitignore-style). Negations only subtract from prior includes; a pattern list consisting only of negations matches nothing and is rejected by lint.

project_tags.description: Literal project handles or semantic tags identifying which projects this skill is relevant to. Absent = ambient / cross-project. A workspace config at `.skill-graph/config.json` may map literal project handles to semantic tag sets so one skill tag matches many projects.

boundary.description: Skills this skill explicitly does NOT own. Items may be bare skill names (v2-compatible) or `{skill, reason}` objects (v3 enrichment). Reasons are strongly recommended because boundary is the anti-ownership field — the reason is what makes the boundary self-documenting.

depends_on.description: Skills this skill requires conceptually or operationally. Items may be bare skill names (v2-compatible) or `{skill, min_version}` objects (v3 enrichment) for version-constrained dependencies.

min_version.description: Semver range for the minimum required version of the target skill. Accepts bare semver (`1.2.0`, treated as exact), caret range (`^1.2.0`), tilde range (`~1.2.0`), comparator range (`>=1.2.0`, `>1.2.0`, `<2.0.0`, `<=1.5.0`), or AND-joined ranges (`>=1.2.0 <2.0.0`). v0.5.0 loosened the pattern from exact-match-only to support real dependency declarations.

lifecycle.description: Per-skill maintenance policy consumed by the drift sentinel.

stale_after_days.description: Days after `drift_check.last_verified` at which the skill is flagged stale. Integration skills rot faster than pure concept skills; pick per skill.

review_cadence.description: How frequently the skill should be re-verified. Drives scheduled drift checks.

runtime_telemetry.description: Optional pointer to a real-world success/failure feed. Consumers may use telemetry to corroborate or override `eval_state`.

feedback_source.description: Path or URL to a JSONL of run receipts. Each receipt is expected to carry `{ timestamp, skill, outcome }` at minimum.
